# Interface Contracts: Merge-PR Skill + Spec-Template SC-Grep Guidance

**Purpose**: Single source of truth for the helper signature, the new skill's flag/arg surface, the canonical diagnostic line shape, the `--check --fix` confirm-never-silent contract, and the three documentation-edit shapes. Implementers MUST match these signatures exactly.

**Generated by**: /plan (Phase 1)
**Consumed by**: /implement (impl-roadmap-and-merge, impl-docs)

## Rules

- Every Bash function/script/skill signature below is the canonical form. Implementer code MUST match flag names, positional ordering, and exit semantics.
- Diagnostic lines are emitted byte-exact per the templates (no trailing whitespace, no embedded newlines). The `step4b-auto-flip:` line MUST be byte-identical to Step 4b.5's pre-extraction emission (NFR-002).
- File mutations are atomic via `mktemp` + `mv` (helper inherits Step 4b.5's invariant).
- Sort orders are explicit; do NOT reorder.

---

## Module A: Shared helper — `auto-flip-on-merge.sh`

### A.1 — `plugin-kiln/scripts/roadmap/auto-flip-on-merge.sh` (NEW — FR-008, FR-009, NFR-002)

```bash
# auto-flip-on-merge.sh — atomic flip of every roadmap item in a PRD's derived_from: list
# from `state: distilled|specced` → `state: shipped` (+ `status: shipped`, + `pr:`, + `shipped_date:`)
# upon confirmed PR merge.
#
# This helper is a verbatim extraction of Step 4b.5 from kiln-build-prd/SKILL.md. The
# extraction is mechanical — no behavior change. NFR-002 (zero-behavior-change) is the
# acceptance gate for this helper.
#
# Usage:
#   bash plugin-kiln/scripts/roadmap/auto-flip-on-merge.sh <pr-number> <prd-path>
#
# Positional arguments:
#   $1  PR_NUMBER  — bare numeric (e.g. "189"). Leading "#" is tolerated and stripped.
#                    Same value the inline Step 4b.5 block read from $PR_NUMBER.
#   $2  PRD_PATH   — repo-relative path to the merged PRD's PRD.md file. Same value the
#                    inline block read from $PRD_PATH.
#
# Behavior (verbatim from Step 4b.5):
#   1. Gate on PR merge state via `gh pr view <pr> --json state,mergedAt`. If `state != MERGED`,
#      emit `step4b-auto-flip: pr-state=<state> auto-flip=skipped items=0 patched=0
#      already_shipped=0 reason=<pr-not-merged|gh-unavailable>` and exit 0.
#   2. Read PRD's `derived_from:` list (uses the `read_derived_from()` helper logic from
#      Step 4b — the helper duplicates that logic inline OR sources it from a shared
#      location; either is acceptable as long as the extracted helper is self-contained
#      and parses YAML the same way).
#   3. For each `.kiln/roadmap/items/*.md` entry that exists on disk:
#      a. If `pr: <PR_NUMBER>` (with optional leading `#`) is already present, increment
#         ALREADY_SHIPPED counter and skip (FR-008 idempotency).
#      b. Else: invoke `bash plugin-kiln/scripts/roadmap/update-item-state.sh "$item" shipped
#         --status shipped` and patch frontmatter to insert `pr: <N>` + `shipped_date:
#         <YYYY-MM-DD>` at the END of the frontmatter (one awk pass + tempfile + mv).
#   4. Emit the canonical diagnostic line (see A.2 below) byte-for-byte.
#
# Output (stdout): exactly ONE diagnostic line per A.2.
# Output (stderr): warnings about MISSING_ENTRIES (entries in derived_from: that don't exist
#                  on disk), one line per missing entry. Does NOT abort.
# Exit: 0 on success (including skipped-due-to-no-merge); non-zero only on usage errors
#       (missing $1 or $2, or $2 not a readable file). Matches Step 4b.5's exit semantics.
```

### A.2 — Canonical diagnostic line (byte-identical to Step 4b.5 — FR-005, NFR-002)

```text
step4b-auto-flip: pr-state=<MERGED|OPEN|CLOSED|unknown> auto-flip=<success|skipped> items=<N> patched=<K> already_shipped=<S> reason=<|no-derived-from|pr-not-merged|gh-unavailable>
```

**Verification regex (the regression fixture asserts this — anchored)**:

```text
^step4b-auto-flip: pr-state=(MERGED|OPEN|CLOSED|unknown) auto-flip=(success|skipped) items=[0-9]+ patched=[0-9]+ already_shipped=[0-9]+ reason=[a-z-]*$
```

**Atomicity invariant**: ONE awk pass rewrites `pr:` and `shipped_date:` into ONE temp file; `mv` is the only commit point per item. No multi-`sed` chains. Inherited from Step 4b.5.

### A.3 — Step 4b.5 refactor (FR-009)

After extraction, `plugin-kiln/skills/kiln-build-prd/SKILL.md` Step 4b.5's bash block (currently ~80 lines starting at the `# FR-003` comment, ending at the closing `echo "step4b-auto-flip: ... reason="` line) MUST be replaced with:

```bash
# FR-001..FR-004 — extracted to shared helper (specs/merge-pr-and-sc-grep-guidance FR-009).
bash plugin-kiln/scripts/roadmap/auto-flip-on-merge.sh "$PR_NUMBER" "$PRD_PATH"
```

The surrounding markdown — `### Step 4b.5: Auto-flip roadmap items on merge` heading, **Purpose**/**When this runs**/**Inputs** prose, **Diagnostic line literal**/**Verification regex** code-fences, **Step 4b.5 invariants** list — is preserved verbatim. Only the Bash code-fence body is replaced. Comment immediately above the new helper invocation cites the FR for traceability.

## Module B: `/kiln:kiln-merge-pr` skill

### B.1 — Skill metadata + flag surface (FR-001, FR-007)

**Skill location**: `plugin-kiln/skills/kiln-merge-pr/SKILL.md`

**Skill frontmatter** (top of file):

```yaml
---
name: kiln-merge-pr
description: Merge a PR and atomically flip its derived_from: roadmap items in one operation. Wraps `gh pr merge` with a mergeability gate, post-merge state confirmation, PRD location, and an invocation of the shared `auto-flip-on-merge.sh` helper. Closes the async-merge auto-flip gap. Flags — `--squash` (default) | `--merge` | `--rebase` for merge method; `--no-flip` to skip the auto-flip stage. Use as `/kiln:kiln-merge-pr <pr-number>`.
---
```

**Argument surface**:

```text
/kiln:kiln-merge-pr <pr-number> [--squash | --merge | --rebase] [--no-flip]
```

- `<pr-number>` — required positional, bare numeric (leading `#` tolerated and stripped).
- `--squash` (default) | `--merge` | `--rebase` — mutually exclusive, propagated to `gh pr merge`.
- `--no-flip` — escape hatch; merge proceeds, auto-flip stage skipped entirely.

### B.2 — End-to-end skill stage contract (FR-002..FR-007, NFR-001, NFR-005)

The skill body executes the following stages sequentially. Each stage emits a diagnostic line on stdout; failures exit non-zero with a human-readable reason.

| Stage | Action | Diagnostic line |
|------:|--------|-----------------|
| 1 | **Working-tree preflight (NFR-005)**: `git status --porcelain` MUST be empty. Refuse if dirty. | `kiln-merge-pr: pr=<n> stage=preflight working-tree=dirty exit=2` |
| 2 | **Mergeability gate (FR-002)**: `gh pr view <pr> --json state,mergeable,mergeStateStatus`. Accept `state=OPEN` AND `mergeStateStatus ∈ {CLEAN, MERGEABLE}`. Idempotent path: if `state=MERGED`, skip Stage 3 and proceed to Stage 4 (FR-002a). Refuse otherwise. | `kiln-merge-pr: pr=<n> stage=gate state=<S> mergeStateStatus=<MS> action=<accept|skip-merge-already-merged|refuse>` |
| 3 | **Merge (FR-003)**: `gh pr merge <pr> --<method> --delete-branch`. Wait for `gh pr view <pr> --json state` to return `MERGED` before proceeding. | `kiln-merge-pr: pr=<n> stage=merge method=<squash|merge|rebase> result=<merged|failed>` |
| 4 | **PRD location (FR-004)**: `gh pr view <pr> --json files`, lexical sort, take `[0]` matching `docs/features/*/PRD.md`. If zero matches, emit `kiln-merge-pr: pr=<n> auto-flip=skipped reason=no-prd-in-changeset` and exit 0. | `kiln-merge-pr: pr=<n> stage=locate-prd prd=<path|none>` |
| 5 | **Auto-flip (FR-005)**: `bash plugin-kiln/scripts/roadmap/auto-flip-on-merge.sh <pr> <prd-path>`. Skipped entirely under `--no-flip` with diagnostic `kiln-merge-pr: pr=<n> auto-flip=skipped reason=--no-flip`. | (Helper emits its own canonical line per A.2.) |
| 6 | **Commit + push (FR-006, NFR-005)**: `git add` ONLY the flipped item paths (one `git add` per file from the helper's stdout list, NEVER `git add -A`). `git commit -m "chore(roadmap): auto-flip on merge of PR #<n>"`. `git push`. | `kiln-merge-pr: pr=<n> stage=commit-and-push files=<count> result=<pushed|skipped-no-changes>` |

**Idempotency (NFR-001, FR-002a)**: re-invocation on an already-merged PR proceeds Stage 2 → skip Stage 3 → Stage 4 → Stage 5 (helper short-circuits to `patched=0 already_shipped=N`) → Stage 6 (no files to add → result `skipped-no-changes`).

**Working-tree-dirty rule (NFR-005, FR-006)**: if Stage 1 detects an unrelated dirty tree, the skill exits non-zero BEFORE the merge runs. The maintainer is told exactly which files are dirty and instructed to clean them. The skill does NOT offer `--stash-and-restore` (PRD R-3, V1 deferral).

### B.3 — Reading the helper's stdout (FR-006 staging precision)

The helper emits one canonical line on stdout (Module A.2). To stage flipped files by exact path (NFR-005, retro #187 PI-1), the skill MUST capture the helper's MUTATED FILE LIST. Two equally valid approaches are permitted:

1. **Re-walk derived_from**: after the helper exits, parse the PRD's `derived_from:` list and `git add` only those `.kiln/roadmap/items/*.md` paths whose `git diff --name-only` shows them mutated.
2. **Helper-emits-paths**: extend the helper to additionally emit `flipped-path: <path>` lines on stderr (one per flipped item), captured by the skill. Acceptable as long as it does NOT change A.2's stdout line.

Implementer chooses one — both satisfy the contract. Approach 1 is preferred for zero-behavior-change to the helper (NFR-002).

---

## Module C: `/kiln:kiln-roadmap --check --fix` (FR-010, FR-011, NFR-004)

### C.1 — Flag surface

`/kiln:kiln-roadmap --check --fix` extends the existing `--check` (escalation-audit FR-005, Check 5: merged-PR drift). Without `--fix`, behavior is byte-identical (NFR-004 backward-compat). With `--fix`, an additional confirm-never-silent stage runs after Check 5's report assembly.

### C.2 — Confirm-never-silent stage

After Check 5 outputs its drift list, when `--fix` is passed:

1. Print: `\nDrift detected: <K> items. Choose action: [fix all / pick / skip]`
2. Read user input. Accept `fix all` | `pick` | `skip` | empty (treated as `skip`, NFR-004).
3. **`skip` / empty**: exit 0 with no writes. `git diff` MUST return empty.
4. **`fix all`**: for each drift entry, resolve PR via `gh pr list --state merged --search "head:<feature-branch>"`. If zero or multiple matches, surface `[ambiguous]` for that entry and skip THAT entry. For unambiguous entries, invoke `bash plugin-kiln/scripts/roadmap/auto-flip-on-merge.sh <pr> <prd>`.
5. **`pick`**: iterate the drift list; per item prompt `[accept / skip]`. Run helper for accepted entries only; same ambiguity-skip rule applies.
6. Print final summary: `fix=success items=<accepted-count> patched=<K> already_shipped=<S> ambiguous=<A>`.

### C.3 — Output shape additions

When `--fix` runs and at least one entry is fixed, the skill emits ONE summary line:

```text
fix=success items=<N-accepted> patched=<K> already_shipped=<S> ambiguous=<A>
```

Plus per-entry helper output (Module A.2 line per fixed item).

### C.4 — Backward-compat invariant (NFR-004)

When `--fix` is absent, the skill's stdout MUST be byte-identical to the pre-FR-010 version. The `--fix` block is gated entirely behind the flag. Implementer asserts via `diff` of `/kiln:kiln-roadmap --check` output before vs after the patch.

---

## Module D: Spec-template authoring note (FR-012, FR-013)

### D.1 — Insertion location

In `plugin-kiln/templates/spec-template.md`, the existing `## Success Criteria *(mandatory)*` section opens with an HTML comment `<!-- ACTION REQUIRED ... -->` and a `### Measurable Outcomes` subsection. The authoring note is appended AFTER `### Measurable Outcomes` (and its example bullet list), as a new subsection clearly labeled.

### D.2 — Authoring-note text (canonical literal — implementer copies verbatim)

```markdown
### Authoring grep-style success criteria against historical state

Grep-style SCs that scan directories with historical state (`.wheel/history/`, `archive/`, `migrations/`, etc.) auto-flag pre-PRD matches every time. The substantive assertion is "new artifacts produced post-PRD have zero matches" — but the bare formulation `git grep -E '<pattern>' .wheel/history/` returns historical noise indistinguishable from a real failure.

Use a **date-bound qualifier** so the SC asserts only on artifacts created since the PRD shipped:

```bash
git log --name-only --pretty='' --since='YYYY-MM-DD' -- '<glob>' | sort -u | xargs -I{} git grep -lE '<pattern>' -- {}
```

Or — preferred when feasible — express the SC against a fresh artifact produced by a consumer-install simulation (the substantive assertion) rather than a directory-wide scan of historical state. A consumer-install fixture under `plugin-<name>/tests/<scenario>/run.sh` is the canonical place to assert "new outputs match this contract" without relying on historical-state filtering.
```

The literal phrase `date-bound qualifier` MUST appear (SC-005 grep target). The recipe code-fence MUST contain `--since='YYYY-MM-DD'` (SC-005 grep target).

---

## Module E: Wheel preprocess — module-level comment + extended tripwire error (FR-014, FR-016)

### E.1 — Module-level comment block insertion

In `plugin-wheel/lib/preprocess.sh`, append a new comment block IMMEDIATELY AFTER the existing module header (which ends near line ~62 with the closing of the substitution-stages narrative) and BEFORE the function definitions. The block reads (canonical literal):

```bash
# Documentary references gotcha (FR-014, specs/merge-pr-and-sc-grep-guidance/spec.md):
#
# Authors who include documentary references to ${WHEEL_PLUGIN_<name>} or
# ${WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_DIR} inside agent `instruction:` text — even purely as prose
# describing legacy behavior — trip two failure modes:
#
#   1. The FR-F4-5 prefix-pattern tripwire fires on grammar variants that the
#      substitution regex skips (e.g., bracketed names containing characters the
#      substitution doesn't recognize), because the tripwire's prefix gate is
#      strictly `${WHEEL_PLUGIN_` or `${WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_DIR`.
#   2. `$$` escaping (`$${WHEEL_PLUGIN_<name>}`) survives the tripwire by design
#      (round-trips to a literal `${WHEEL_PLUGIN_<name>}` in the rendered output),
#      but that literal then lands in `.wheel/history/success/*.json` archives
#      where the SC-F-6 archive-grep tripwire trips it from the OTHER direction.
#
# The durable rule for documentation prose is: do NOT reproduce the token grammar
# verbatim. Substitute plain prose (e.g., "the workflow's plugin directory" or
# "the calling plugin's WHEEL_PLUGIN_<name> entry") that does not contain the
# `${...}` shape at all. This is the rule consumers see in
# plugin-wheel/README.md §Writing agent instructions.
```

The literal word `documentary` MUST appear in the comment (SC-006 grep target).

### E.2 — FR-F4-5 tripwire error-text extension (FR-016)

The current FR-F4-5 tripwire error text (emitted via `printf` near line ~177 of `preprocess.sh`) ends with the residual-token report. The patch is to APPEND a single line to the error text:

```text
If you intended this as documentary text, rewrite as plain prose; the tripwire fires on the prefix pattern even with $$ escaping.
```

Implementation: extend the existing `printf` format string with a `\n` separator and the new sentence, OR add a second `printf` immediately after, before the `exit 1`. The literal sentence MUST appear in the rendered stderr output for any tripwire violation (SC-006 verification).

### E.3 — Length consideration (OQ-2)

If consumer log-parsing trips on the longer error, condense to:

```text
If documentary, see plugin-wheel/README.md §Writing agent instructions.
```

Implementer chooses the longer or condensed form at edit time. Both satisfy FR-016 (the literal "documentary" sentinel survives). Prefer the longer form unless a concrete consumer is identified.

---

## Module F: Wheel README "Writing agent instructions" section (FR-015)

### F.1 — Insertion location

In `plugin-wheel/README.md`, add a new top-level heading `## Writing agent instructions` AFTER the existing `## Workflow Format` block (which currently ends near the field-table and step-types description) and BEFORE any later section (the README is short — 293 lines — so the new section anchors near the end is acceptable; specifier did not enumerate every later heading).

### F.2 — Section text (canonical literal — implementer copies verbatim, may add prose around it)

```markdown
## Writing agent instructions

When authoring `instruction:` text for an `agent`-type step, do **not** reproduce the wheel's token grammar verbatim — even as documentary prose describing legacy behavior. The two reserved prefixes — `${WHEEL_PLUGIN_<name>}` and `${WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_DIR}` — are policed by two tripwires that fire from different directions:

- The FR-F4-5 prefix-pattern tripwire in `lib/preprocess.sh` fires on any post-substitution residual token. Grammar variants the substitution regex skips still trip the tripwire on the next workflow run.
- The `$$` escape (`$${WHEEL_PLUGIN_<name>}`) survives the tripwire intentionally — it round-trips to a literal `${WHEEL_PLUGIN_<name>}` in the rendered output. That literal then lands in `.wheel/history/success/*.json` archives where the SC-F-6 archive-grep tripwire trips it.

The durable rule for **documentary** references in `instruction:` text is: rewrite as plain prose that does not reproduce the token grammar at all. Examples:

- ✅ "the workflow's plugin directory"
- ✅ "the calling plugin's plugin-dir registry entry"
- ❌ `${WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_DIR}` (even with `$$` escaping)

If you genuinely need the token to be substituted at workflow-run time, leave it unescaped; the substitution stage will replace it before the tripwire scans. The rule above applies only to prose that DESCRIBES the token without wanting it substituted.
```

The literal word `documentary` MUST appear in the section (SC-006 grep target).

---

## Module G: Test fixture — `auto-flip-on-merge-fixture/run.sh` (NFR-002, SC-002)

### G.1 — Fixture structure

```text
plugin-kiln/tests/auto-flip-on-merge-fixture/
├── run.sh                     # Entry point — sets up tmpdir, invokes helper, asserts
├── golden/
│   ├── prd.md                 # Snapshot of docs/features/2026-04-26-escalation-audit/PRD.md
│   │                          # at commit 22a91b10's parent (pre-merge state)
│   ├── pre/
│   │   ├── <item-1>.md        # Pre-merge frontmatter (state: distilled, no pr:, no shipped_date:)
│   │   ├── <item-2>.md
│   │   └── <item-3>.md
│   └── post/
│       ├── <item-1>.md        # Post-merge frontmatter (commit 22a91b10's state)
│       ├── <item-2>.md
│       └── <item-3>.md
```

The three items captured are the three `derived_from:` items from `docs/features/2026-04-26-escalation-audit/PRD.md`. Implementer captures pre/post snapshots via `git show 22a91b10^:.kiln/roadmap/items/<item>.md` (pre) and `git show 22a91b10:.kiln/roadmap/items/<item>.md` (post).

### G.2 — Fixture run.sh contract

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# plugin-kiln/tests/auto-flip-on-merge-fixture/run.sh
# Citing: SC-002 (specs/merge-pr-and-sc-grep-guidance/spec.md)
set -euo pipefail

TMP="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$TMP"' EXIT
HERE="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"

# 1. Scaffold a fake repo root in $TMP with .kiln/roadmap/items/ containing the three pre-merge snapshots
mkdir -p "$TMP/.kiln/roadmap/items"
cp "$HERE/golden/pre/"*.md "$TMP/.kiln/roadmap/items/"
mkdir -p "$TMP/docs/features/2026-04-26-escalation-audit"
cp "$HERE/golden/prd.md" "$TMP/docs/features/2026-04-26-escalation-audit/PRD.md"

# 2. Stub `gh` to return MERGED for the pinned PR number (189)
mkdir -p "$TMP/bin"
cat > "$TMP/bin/gh" <<'STUB'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
case "$*" in
  *"--json state"*) echo '{"state":"MERGED","mergedAt":"2026-04-26T00:00:00Z"}'; exit 0 ;;
  *) echo '{}'; exit 0 ;;
esac
STUB
chmod +x "$TMP/bin/gh"

# 3. Run the helper from $TMP, with stubbed gh on PATH
( cd "$TMP" && PATH="$TMP/bin:$PATH" \
  bash "$REPO_ROOT/plugin-kiln/scripts/roadmap/auto-flip-on-merge.sh" 189 \
       "docs/features/2026-04-26-escalation-audit/PRD.md" )

# 4. Diff each item against golden post snapshot — MUST be byte-identical
for item in "$HERE/golden/post/"*.md; do
  diff -u "$TMP/.kiln/roadmap/items/$(basename "$item")" "$item" \
    || { echo "FAIL: $(basename "$item") byte-diff vs golden/post"; exit 1; }
done

# 5. Re-run the helper; assert idempotency (no further mutation)
( cd "$TMP" && PATH="$TMP/bin:$PATH" \
  bash "$REPO_ROOT/plugin-kiln/scripts/roadmap/auto-flip-on-merge.sh" 189 \
       "docs/features/2026-04-26-escalation-audit/PRD.md" )

for item in "$HERE/golden/post/"*.md; do
  diff -u "$TMP/.kiln/roadmap/items/$(basename "$item")" "$item" \
    || { echo "FAIL: $(basename "$item") byte-diff after idempotent re-run"; exit 1; }
done

echo "PASS"
```

### G.3 — Wiring into kiln-test harness

The fixture follows the existing convention under `plugin-kiln/tests/<name>/run.sh`. Discovery is automatic — `/kiln:kiln-test plugin-kiln auto-flip-on-merge-fixture` invokes it. CI runs the full plugin-kiln test directory (existing convention).

---

## Module H: Reused (no signature changes)

- `plugin-kiln/scripts/roadmap/update-item-state.sh` — reused with `--status` flag (escalation-audit FR-002). No new flags.
- `plugin-kiln/scripts/roadmap/list-items.sh` / `parse-item-frontmatter.sh` — reused verbatim.
- Existing `--check` Checks 1–5 — unchanged. `--fix` is purely additive.
- Existing Step 4b.5 markdown surrounding prose (`### Step 4b.5: ...` heading, **Purpose**, **When this runs**, **Inputs**, **Diagnostic line literal**, **Verification regex**, **Step 4b.5 invariants**) — preserved verbatim during the FR-009 refactor.
